description: This group contains two scanning electron microscopy (SEM) micrographs each with a 10 μm scale bar, presenting microstructures of hot-rolled air-cooled Fe–8.03Mn–6.10Al–0.4C (wt%) low-density duplex steel after annealing at 800 °C for different durations. The first micrograph corresponds to the sample annealed for 5 min, which consists of tempered martensite, austenite and δ-ferrite, with clear twin crystal structure in martensite. The second micrograph corresponds to the sample annealed for 15 min, where partial prior martensite degenerates into needle-like α-ferrite, and the austenite films between α-ferrite and/or prior martensite become thickened, indicating increased austenite fraction with longer annealing time. These observations demonstrate the microstructure evolution rule under varied annealing duration, and support the conclusion that the formed needle-like α-ferrite refines austenite grains and enhances austenite mechanical stability, which benefits the good strength-ductility match of the air-cooled annealed steel.
